injuryUpdates.injuryType: High-grade partial tear of medial cruciate ligament (no surgery required)
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Targeting June 6 friendly vs Honduras; on track for June 16 tournament opener

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Suffered MCL tear in collision with goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky vs Sunderland

injuryUpdates.source: OneFootball, World Soccer Talk, Sports Mole - May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Status upgraded from major_doubt to doubt: AFA sent a physiotherapist to London to monitor rehab, PRP treatment underway, no surgery needed. Romero included in 55-man preliminary squad and is targeting the June 6 (Honduras) and June 9 (Iceland) warm-ups. Tottenham season likely over, but tournament participation now considered likely.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Recurring soleus muscle strain (left leg) — recovered
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Returned for Inter Milan vs Lazio in early May 2026

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Returned to starting lineup after second soleus injury, has been leading Serie A scoring charts with 16 goals

injuryUpdates.source: Sempre Inter, ESPN, beIN Sports - April/May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Aggravated soleus muscle vs Como on 2026-04-10 after returning from a 7-week absence. Returned in early May for Inter and named in 55-man preliminary squad. Recurring nature of muscle injuries makes him a minor concern heading into the tournament; failed to score in 2022 tournament run due to ankle issue.

squadChanges.details: Confirmed firm in his retirement from the national team, ruling out a return for the tournament. Excluded from the 55-man preliminary squad announced May 12.
Shock exclusion from Scaloni's 55-man preliminary squad despite no announced international retirement. Part of Scaloni's generational transition.
Shock exclusion from Scaloni's 55-man preliminary squad. No retirement announcement; left out as part of the generational refresh.
Chose not to return to the national team; excluded from the 55-man preliminary squad.
Hamburger SV midfielder receives his first official Argentina call-up, included in the 55-man preliminary squad.

Included in the 55-man preliminary squad despite a six-match ban for homophobic insults against Vinicius Junior in the Champions League. The ban could sideline him for Argentina's first two group matches.

bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Argentina are 1/3 to top Group J and 1/100 to qualify from the group, reflecting their runaway-favourites status in Group J vs Algeria, Austria, Jordan. Tournament-winner odds (8/1) place them behind the front-runners (Spain and France ~+450) but in the second tier of contenders.

summary: Argentina played no international matches in the window, but the May 12 announcement of Scaloni's 55-man preliminary roster reshapes the picture: Angel Di Maria and Franco Armani confirmed out via retirement, while Paulo Dybala and Angel Correa were dropped as part of a generational refresh. The biggest fitness news is positive — Cristian Romero's MCL tear does not require surgery and he is now targeting the June 6 warm-up vs Honduras, upgrading his status from major doubt to doubt, while Lautaro Martinez returned for Inter Milan in early May after a soleus re-injury and remains a minor recurring-injury concern.
